title: Front Panel Controls diagram (Home/Back buttons, LCD display, control knobs, power switch, MIG gun socket, positive socket)
Specifications page: MIG/TIG/Stick duty cycle and power tables for 120V and 240V
Quick Start Guide page 1: wire spool loading, feed mechanism, contact tip/nozzle, feed tension steps 1-6
Stick cable setup: ground clamp to negative terminal, electrode holder to positive terminal
MIG cable setup (ground clamp negative, wire feed power positive) and Flux-Cored setup note (reversed polarity)
TIG cable setup: ground clamp to positive terminal, TIG torch to negative terminal, gas line, foot pedal
How to choose a welding process: skill level, shielding gas, material, thickness, applications, weld cleanliness matrix for Flux-Cored/MIG/Stick/TIG

Interior Controls / wire feed mechanism: wire spool, spool knob, wire feed mechanism, feed tensioner, idler arm, feed roller knob, wire inlet liner, cold wire feed switch, foot pedal socket, wire feed control socket
1-2 lb wire spool loading: wingnut, spacer, spool spindle, spool brake pad, welder wall stack-up
10-12 lb wire spool loading: spool knob, wingnut, spacer, 10-12 lb spool adapter, spool spindle, brake pad
Feed tensioner and idler arm: loosening the tensioner knob so the spring-loaded idler arm swings up
Feed roller removal and replacement (steps A-D): feed roller knob, solid core V-groove sizes 0.025/0.030/0.035, flux-cored knurled groove sizes 0.030/0.035/0.045
Threading welding wire from the spool through the wire inlet liner and feed guide (hold wire securely)
MIG gun parts: nozzle and contact tip removal
Rated duty cycle clock graphics: 120VAC 40% at 100A (4 min welding / 6 min resting, 100% at 75A) and 240VAC 25% at 200A (2.5 min welding / 7.5 min resting, 100% at 115A)
Clamping the ground clamp to bare metal on the workpiece (ground connection depends on desired welding polarity)
TIG torch assembly: back cap, collet, collet body, ceramic nozzle, tungsten electrode insertion point
Sharpening the tungsten electrode on a grinding wheel: grind parallel to electrode length, conical tip 2.5x electrode diameter

Wire weld diagnosis: penetration profiles (inadequate/proper/excess) and example wire weld photos - good weld, voltage too low/high, wire feed too slow/fast, travel speed too fast/slow, CTWD too long or wrong polarity, with corrections
Wire weld penetration profile views (burn-through / proper / inadequate), bend at joint, coat of slag over flux-cored weld, weld not adhering - causes and solutions
Wire weld defect photos with causes and solutions: porosity (small cavities/holes in bead), excessive spatter, crooked/wavy bead, burn-through
Stick weld diagnosis: penetration heat control and example stick weld photos - good weld, current too low/high, weld speed too fast/slow, arc length too short/long, with corrections
Exploded assembly diagram with numbered parts 1-61 (see parts list on page 46 for part names)
